primary_variables.matched_definition: Addition of metabolites or metabolic substrates to alter pathway activity.
evidence: controlled longitudinal weight perturbation study combining multiple omics strategies (genomics, transcriptomics, multiple proteomics assays, metabolomics and microbiomics) during periods of weight gain and loss in humans
subjects were then placed on a controlled weight gain diet for 30 days by adding an average of 880 calories/day in addition to their usual daily intake
subjects then underwent supervised weight loss for 6–9 weeks (average 60 days) such that they returned to their baseline weight

primary_variables.matched_definition: Comparison between disease and matched control cohorts.
evidence: compare equally-obese individuals who are either IR or insulin sensitive (IS) to characterize differences between these metabolically-disparate groups
Thirteen fasted overweight IR participants (defined by SSPG ≥ 150 mg/dl, Table S1 and Materials and Methods); and ten BMI-matched healthy overweight IS participants completed the study

attributes.dose_or_concentration: average of 880 calories/day in addition to their usual daily intake
evidence: subjects were then placed on a controlled weight gain diet for 30 days by adding an average of 880 calories/day in addition to their usual daily intake
Exact caloric excess, administered by research dietitian in the form of snacks/beverages
subjects then underwent supervised weight loss for 6–9 weeks (average 60 days) such that they returned to their baseline weight

attributes.route_or_context: T1: baseline, T2: peak weight, T3: post weight loss, T4: follow-up
evidence: sampled 23 carefully selected healthy participants with BMI 25–35 kg/m2 at baseline before perturbation and during periods of short-term weight gain followed by weight-loss, with samples drawn and metabolic measurements made at baseline (T1), peak weight (T2) and post weight loss (T3) (Fig. 1), and after three months of weight stability following return to baseline weight (T4)

multiplex_channel_hints: labeled with 10-plex Thermo Scientific™ Tandem Mass Tag™ (TMT) Reagents
divided into groups of nine and pooled together with a master reference sample to create 10-plexed samples

evidence: Comparing omics profiles at peak weight to baseline profiles
compared omics profiles assayed after weight loss to those of the previous peak weight sample
identify analytes that differ between the two groups (insulin resistant and insulin sensitive)

base_candidates_ranked.reason: The experiment explicitly uses 10-plex Tandem Mass Tag (TMT) reagents and data-dependent acquisition (DDA) on an LTQ Orbitrap Elite.
The experiment explicitly uses 10-plex Tandem Mass Tag (TMT) reagents and data-dependent acquisition (DDA) on an LTQ Orbitrap Elite.
